VAL-IDX-006.evidence: Fastify repo (250,578 LOC, 381 files, 2896 chunks) indexed in 28.74s wall-clock with remote embedding API. Index size 18.7MB vs 27.1MB source = 0.67x ratio. Well under both 120s wall-clock and 60s parsing+storage targets.
VAL-IDX-006.details: After performance-fix-core-engine (batch_size increase from 64 to 128+, reduced rate limit backoff from 6s to 2s), indexing time dropped dramatically from prior round's 96.54s to 28.74s. The fix addressed the embedding API batching bottleneck. Full output: files_parsed=381, chunks_created=2896, embeddings_generated=2896, binary_skipped=1, elapsed_secs=28.74.

VAL-RET-011.evidence: BM25-only p95 latency: 3ms (target: <10ms) measured on 25 queries. Cached hybrid local-only components: BM25=2ms + vector_search=4ms + RRF_fusion<1ms = ~7ms total (target: <100ms). First-time hybrid (cold cache, no reranker): p50=895ms, p95=1340ms (API-dependent, expected). First-time hybrid (with reranker): p50=3850ms, p95=4957ms (dual API calls). Unit tests confirm cache hit latency <50ms.
VAL-RET-011.details: The performance-fix-core-engine feature added an LRU CachedEmbeddingProvider that caches query_text -> embedding_vector in memory. Cache hits bypass the remote API entirely. Debug log analysis shows the non-API components of hybrid search (BM25 + vector + RRF) take ~7ms total. The embedding API call adds ~850ms per cold query. 7 unit tests for the cache mechanism all pass. BM25 fallback is always available for low-latency requirements (p95=3ms). Per-process CLI cache means cache benefits apply to MCP server (persistent process) or programmatic library usage. The assertion text acknowledges 'First-time hybrid query latency depends on embedding API provider.'

frictions: Cache is per-process (in-memory LRU), so CLI invocations each start with a cold cache. Cache benefits are most relevant for MCP server or library usage where the process persists.
